text.title: Factory Functions and factory_boy
text.summary: Factory patterns with keyword-only overrides, sequenced determinism, and builder pattern for complex test objects.

rule.statement: Factory functions must use keyword-only arguments with explicit defaults. Never positional arguments.
rule.rationale: Positional factory arguments drift silently — adding a parameter in the middle breaks all callers. Keyword-only args force explicit naming at call sites.

rule.statement: Use sequenced determinism by default: `factory.Sequence(lambda n: f"user{n}@example.com")` not `Faker('email')` for fields used in snapshot assertions.
rule.rationale: Random Faker values produce different output on each run, breaking snapshot tests. Sequences produce `user0@example.com`, `user1@example.com`, etc. — deterministic across runs.

example.code:
# Keyword-only factory with explicit defaults def make_user( *, name: str = "Test User", email: Optional[str] = None, role: str = "user", **kwargs ) -> User: return User( id=str(uuid4()), name=name, email=email or f"user-{uuid4().hex[:8]}@example.com", role=role, **kwargs )
# Usage: always keyword args user = make_user(name="Alice", role="admin")

import factory from factory import Faker, Sequence, SubFactory
class UserFactory(factory.django.DjangoModelFactory): class Meta: model = User
id = factory.LazyFunction(uuid4) email = Sequence(lambda n: f"user{n}@example.com") # Deterministic name = Faker("name") role = "user"
class Params: admin = factory.Trait(role="admin")
class OrderFactory(factory.django.DjangoModelFactory): class Meta: model = Order
user = SubFactory(UserFactory) status = "pending" total = Faker("pydecimal", min_value=10, max_value=1000)
# Usage user = UserFactory(admin=True) # Admin trait order = OrderFactory(user=user) # Explicit user
example.note: Traits encapsulate variant configurations. SubFactory creates related objects automatically.

section.title: Builder Pattern
section.p: Use the builder pattern for complex objects with many optional attributes, especially when different test scenarios need different combinations.
